page: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It also includes 5669 GB of bundled local storage and is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The server achieved a top-tier PassMark CPU score of 106,546.32 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 97,236.44. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with read-only cached small block speeds reaching 7,269.34 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 348.8 GB/sec. Database operations and key-value store performance are also in the top tier, with Redis SET operations reaching 156.15 million operations per second and PassMark database operations scoring 33,116.64. For language model workloads, the CPU's AMX instructions enable top-tier prompt processing speeds of 312.2 tokens per second on 7B models, though text generation speeds remain average at 35.65 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D96ds_v7 offers high resource density and balanced compute-to-memory ratios, making it highly efficient for consolidated workloads without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. However, the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, despite strong CPU-based inference capabilities. The massive 5669 GB local storage and high database performance make this server highly suitable for relational databases, high-throughput web serving, large-scale Redis deployments, and enterprise-grade general-purpose applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, 5669 GB of bundled local storage, and supports Premium Storage. The architecture includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which drive top-tier performance in database operations, Redis key-value transactions, and multi-threaded decompression. Benchmarks show exceptional memory read bandwidth of up to 7269.34 GB/sec and strong multi-core CPU capabilities. Lacking a dedicated GPU, it relies on its high-core Intel processor for compute tasks, making it highly efficient for high-throughput web serving, large databases, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v7 is a 96-vCPU general-purpose server featuring an Intel Xeon processor, 384 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, delivering top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v7 is a 96-vCPU general-purpose server with 384 GB RAM, 5669 GB local storage, and top-tier database performance.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose server featuring massive local storage, exceptional memory bandwidth, and advanced Intel AMX acceleration for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory paired with 5669 GB of bundled local storage
Top-tier database performance and Redis throughput exceeding 156 million operations per second
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support accelerating CPU-based machine learning inference
Best suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and enterprise applications
